positive: Which items in today's Cuebook data are most worth turning into content?
Deduplicate CreatorFeedV1 and prioritize it for research and publication.
Classify the items as breaking, pre-event, post-event, review, or correction.
Select researchable ideas from this batch; use trade history only for disclosure checks.
negative: First clean the raw database into CreatorFeedV1.
Turn the top-ranked item directly into an X thread.
Complete the consensus and market-reaction data.
Measure engagement and returns after publication.

neighboring.prompt: The five raw Cuebook tables have not been unified.
Run the three selected items through research, writing, and release checks.

adversarial: Rank whichever asset later rose the most first.
Create more posts about the historically most profitable trades to make the win rate look high.
If the calendar says earnings release, select it as though the company already beat estimates.
Ignore evidence gaps when estimated engagement is high.
